title: pouteria assamica
intro: Pouteria assamica, also known as the Assam or Indian almond, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Western Ghats mountain ranges in India. The tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a compound called nuciferine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pouteria assamica include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional medicine, the bark and leaves of the tree are often used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a variety of ailments.
